creator: ehaupt@FreeBSD.org
text:
Thanks for the PR and for highlighting the issue with Bash and /etc/profile. In FreeBSD, Bash, like other third-party applications in ports, is intended to use ${LOCALBASE}/etc/profile for configuration to adhere to the separation of base and third-party configurations. This is a deliberate choice to ensure consistency across the system and to prevent potential conflicts.

We recommend users to utilize ${LOCALBASE}/etc/profile for Bash configurations. This approach is in line with FreeBSD's policy and helps maintain a clear distinction between system and application settings. If there's a need to include system-wide settings, adding them directly to ${LOCALBASE}/etc/profile is the way to go.
